rules: [P1] Do not infer missing data from broader time windows; each required time window must be queried explicitly.
[P1] Filter Immunization records by vaccine code and sort by occurrence date descending to find the most recent dose.
[P1] If a required observation returns zero records, report missing data and do not trigger protocol actions.
[P1] Never fabricate or assume clinical values; base all decisions solely on retrieved data.
[P1] Compare the retrieved lab value to the protocol's normal range; if within range, report no action required.
[P1] After creating any new resource (e.g., ServiceRequest, MedicationRequest, Procedure), immediately re‑query it to confirm successful creation and correct content.
[P1] When multiple possible codes exist, query each code separately (or combine if the API permits) using distinct API calls.
[P1] If the API lacks a sort parameter, fetch records and programmatically sort descending to pick the first entry.
[P1] Issue exactly one API request per turn as the first line, using the prescribed JSON format or raw GET URL, with no commentary.
[P1] After each API call, verify the response status before proceeding.
[P1] If the required medication code is unknown, first query the medication/terminology service to locate the correct code before creating or updating an order.
[P1] When verifying prior clinical events (e.g., vaccinations), query Immunization and Procedure resources using appropriate codes before assuming absence.
[P1] After any update (e.g., cancel/discontinue) to a resource, immediately re‑query that resource to verify the status change.

[P1] Before creating a new order of a given type (e.g., imaging), query prior Procedure resources with matching codes, evaluate the most recent occurrence against the required time window, and create the order only if none exists or the prior one is outdated.
[P1] Never finish until all required API calls have succeeded and the final state has been verified by a re‑query.
[P1] When retrieving resources that may be paginated, follow pagination links or bundle entries to collect the complete result set before local processing.
[P1] Summarize findings, actions, and final counts (including aggregates) in the FINISH statement; avoid extra reasoning unless explicitly requested.
[P1] Reuse previously filtered datasets for multiple calculations to avoid redundant requests.
[P1] If the post‑update query shows the resource unchanged, repeat the update or use the correct API endpoint/method.
[P1] After computing an aggregate, re‑query the same filter to confirm the result matches before reporting.
[P1] Query active orders of the target type, create if none, report status if one, discontinue all but the newest if multiple, and verify only one active order remains.
[P1] Validate and verify all identifiers (patient, encounter, order IDs) before any query or action.

[P1] Use the system's current ISO‑formatted datetime (including timezone) for all temporal comparisons, applying relative windows (e.g., now‑6h) and verifying resource existence before applying time‑based thresholds.

[P1] Always query necessary resources (Observation, MedicationRequest, Device, ServiceRequest, Procedure) with patient ID and appropriate filters (status, codes, date‑range); sort by primary timestamp descending and use the most recent record(s).
[P1] For aggregates, retrieve all matching records, compute the metric, and report numeric results with units; report “no data” only when a query returns zero records.
